⚡ Daily Challenge ▼
Grade 6 · Math
A recipe calls for 3 cups of flour for every 2 cups of sugar. If you use 9 cups of flour, how many cups of sugar do you need?
💡 Think: set up a proportion. 3/2 = 9/? Cross-multiply to solve.
6 cups of sugar. Scale factor: 9 ÷ 3 = 3, so 2 × 3 = 6.
